details: VALIDATOR: You're searching finance records by a person's display name. Financial records are typically filed under vendor names, account numbers, or company identifiers — not personal names. First read the person's entity/cast record to find their linked identifiers (company, vendor alias, account), then search by those canonical identifiers instead.

function.content:
---
record_type: outbound_email
created_at: 2026-03-02T18:21:00Z
send_state: draft
to:
  - miles@novak.example
subject: Invoice bundle for Nina (oldest 4)
attachments:
  - 50_finance/invoices/2026_01_17__eur_000890__inv_0004__northstar_design_partner_sprint.md
  - 50_finance/invoices/2025_12_01__eur_000530__inv_0003__northstar_backfill_gamma.md
  - 50_finance/invoices/2025_11_03__eur_000770__inv_0002__northstar_backfill_beta.md
  - 50_finance/invoices/2025_10_06__eur_000610__inv_0001__northstar_backfill_alpha.md
---
Hi Miles,

Attached are the oldest 4 invoices linked to Nina.

Best,
Miles

next_step.current_state: Outbox write completed. Need post-write verification with evidence; read the written outbox file for confirmation and grounding, then delete processed inbox item and report completion.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Read outbound email file for verification
next_step.observation: An outbound email draft file was written successfully to outbox.

next_step.current_state: Verified outbox file content: recipient matches canonical Miles email; attachments are all previously read invoice files and ordered newest-first among selected oldest-4 set (2026-01-17 > 2025-12-01 > 2025-11-03 > 2025-10-06). Now perform cleanup by deleting processed inbox item.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Submit completion report with evidence refs
next_step.observation: Read-back confirms outbound draft exists with four Nina-linked invoice attachments and expected recipient.

next_step.current_state: Pre-submit verification: Task was to handle next inbox item. I processed lowest inbox file, verified sender identity against canonical entity record, identified all Nina-linked invoices from canonical invoice records (5 total) and selected oldest 4 by issued_on date, created outbound draft in outbox with attachments ordered newest-first per outbox rule, then deleted the processed inbox file. No missing requested action.
next_step.observation: Processed inbox item has been deleted after creating the requested reply draft.

function.message: Handled the next inbox item by preparing a reply draft to Miles with the oldest 4 invoices linked to Nina attached, then deleted the processed inbox file.
function.rulebook_notes: Followed mandatory bootstrap (tree /, read AGENTS.MD, context), inspected 99_system and workflows before task work, treated inbox header as canonical transport metadata, verified sender via canonical primary_contact_email, used invoice records as canonical linkage evidence, wrote outbound email in outbox schema, and deleted inbox item only after completion.
function.outcome_justification: Inbox request in 00_inbox/000_invoice-bundle-request.md asked for oldest 4 invoices linked to Nina. Canonical sender identity matched (10_entities/cast/miles.md). Nina linkage was confirmed directly in five invoice records via related_entity field; oldest four by issued_on are inv_0001 (2025-10-06), inv_0002 (2025-11-03), inv_0003 (2025-12-01), inv_0004 (2026-01-17). Draft reply was created at 60_outbox/outbox/eml_2026-03-02T18-21-00Z.md with those four attachments (ordered newest-first per outbox rule), and inbox item was deleted.
completed_steps_laconic: read entity records for Miles and Nina
searched and read all Nina-linked invoices
read back outbox draft for verification
